prompt: Dashboards for our low-traffic services are minutes stale. The metrics flusher is configured to ship at least every 200ms, but services that emit a slow trickle of points basically never get a flush — the batch just keeps growing until it hits the size cap (which for a quiet service can take half an hour) or the process shuts down. High-traffic services look fine because they hit the cap constantly, so nobody noticed until the on-call stared at a frozen graph during an incident. flusher.go is the whole thing. Fix it, leave flusher_test.go alone, prove with `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...`.
